rag_rules: Generate guarantee clauses for behavior relations using AGREE implication syntax with visible identifiers.
Use nested if-then-else for branching logic in equations or guarantees when conditions are mutually exclusive.
generic_rules: Map requirement intent (e.g., guarantee) directly to AGREE clause type.
Use only identifiers present in the visible context to avoid unresolved references.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use => for implication, and/or/not for logical operators in expressions.
Declare auxiliary variables with eq name : type = expression; when needed for clarity.
Use int, real, or bool as scalar types; never integer, boolean, or AADL property names.
anti_patterns: Do not embed AADL property names (e.g., Period, Deadline) as variables in AGREE expressions.
Do not use assign in implementation scope for guarantees — use eq or guarantee instead.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
